title: Multiply complex numbers
description: Given two complex numbers, find their product.
topic_path: Module 1: Polynomial, rational, and radical relationships
Topic D: Lesson 37: Complex number operations

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{-4-5i}) \cdot (\blue{1-i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{-4-5i}) \cdot (\blue{1-i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{1}) + (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{-i}) + (\pink{-5i} \cdot \blue{1}) + (\pink{-5i} \cdot \blue{-i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (-4) + (4i) + (-5i) + (5i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad -4 + (4 - 5)i + 5 i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ -4 + (4 - 5)i - 5 $
The result is simplified: $ (-4 - 5) + (-1i) = -9-i $

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{-4-4i}) \cdot (\blue{-5-3i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{-4-4i}) \cdot (\blue{-5-3i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{-5}) + (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{-3i}) + (\pink{-4i} \cdot \blue{-5}) + (\pink{-4i} \cdot \blue{-3i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (20) + (12i) + (20i) + (12i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad 20 + (12 + 20)i + 12 i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ 20 + (12 + 20)i - 12 $
The result is simplified: $ (20 - 12) + (32i) = 8+32i $

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{-4+2i}) \cdot (\blue{4-4i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{-4+2i}) \cdot (\blue{4-4i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{4}) + (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{-4i}) + (\pink{2i} \cdot \blue{4}) + (\pink{2i} \cdot \blue{-4i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (-16) + (16i) + (8i) + (-8i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad -16 + (16 + 8)i - 8 i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ -16 + (16 + 8)i - (-8) $
The result is simplified: $ (-16 + 8) + (24i) = -8+24i $

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{-4+4i}) \cdot (\blue{3+2i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{-4+4i}) \cdot (\blue{3+2i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{3}) + (\pink{-4} \cdot \blue{2i}) + (\pink{4i} \cdot \blue{3}) + (\pink{4i} \cdot \blue{2i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (-12) + (-8i) + (12i) + (8i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad -12 + (-8 + 12)i + 8 i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ -12 + (-8 + 12)i - 8 $
The result is simplified: $ (-12 - 8) + (4i) = -20+4i $

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{-3-2i}) \cdot (\blue{-4+2i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{-3-2i}) \cdot (\blue{-4+2i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{-3} \cdot \blue{-4}) + (\pink{-3} \cdot \blue{2i}) + (\pink{-2i} \cdot \blue{-4}) + (\pink{-2i} \cdot \blue{2i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (12) + (-6i) + (8i) + (-4i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad 12 + (-6 + 8)i - 4 i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ 12 + (-6 + 8)i - (-4) $
The result is simplified: $ (12 + 4) + (2i) = 16+2i $

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{-3-i}) \cdot (\blue{3+i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{-3-i}) \cdot (\blue{3+i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{-3} \cdot \blue{3}) + (\pink{-3} \cdot \blue{i}) + (\pink{-i} \cdot \blue{3}) + (\pink{-i} \cdot \blue{i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (-9) + (-3i) + (-3i) + (-i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad -9 + (-3 - 3)i - i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ -9 + (-3 - 3)i - (-1) $
The result is simplified: $ (-9 + 1) + (-6i) = -8-6i $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ -3 + (3 + 2)i - (-2) $
The result is simplified: $ (-3 + 2) + (5i) = -1+5i $

Then simplify the terms:

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{5-5i}) \cdot (\blue{-3+5i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{5-5i}) \cdot (\blue{-3+5i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{5} \cdot \blue{-3}) + (\pink{5} \cdot \blue{5i}) + (\pink{-5i} \cdot \blue{-3}) + (\pink{-5i} \cdot \blue{5i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (-15) + (25i) + (15i) + (-25i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad -15 + (25 + 15)i - 25 i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ -15 + (25 + 15)i - (-25) $
The result is simplified: $ (-15 + 25) + (40i) = 10+40i $

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply and simplify the following complex numbers:**

 $(\pink{5+2i}) \cdot (\blue{-5-i})$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Complex numbers are multiplied like any two binomials.

First use the distributive property:

  $\qquad (\pink{5+2i}) \cdot (\blue{-5-i}) = $
 
 $ \qquad \qquad (\pink{5} \cdot \blue{-5}) + (\pink{5} \cdot \blue{-i}) + (\pink{2i} \cdot \blue{-5}) + (\pink{2i} \cdot \blue{-i}) $

Then simplify the terms:

 $ \qquad (-25) + (-5i) + (-10i) + (-2i^2) $

Imaginary unit multiples can be grouped together.

 $ \qquad -25 + (-5 - 10)i - 2 i^2 $

After we plug in $i^2 = -1$, the result becomes

 $ -25 + (-5 - 10)i - (-2) $
The result is simplified: $ (-25 + 2) + (-15i) = -23-15i $
